heading: Permissible purposes of consumer reports
label: 1681b Permissible purposes of consumer reports
notes: Amendment of Subsection (c), §§ 2(a), 3, , , 494, provided that, effective on the date that is 180 days after , subsection (c) of this section is amended by adding at the end the following:Pub. L. 119–36139 Stat. 493Sept. 5, 2025Sept. 5, 2025(4) Treatment of prescreening report requests(A) DefinitionsIn this paragraph:(i) Credit unionThe term “credit union” means a Federal credit union or a State credit union, as those terms are defined, respectively, in section 101 of the Federal Credit Union Act ().12 U.S.C. 1752(ii) Insured depository institutionThe term “insured depository institution” has the meaning given the term in section 3 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act ().12 U.S.C. 1813(c)(iii) Residential mortgage loanThe term “residential mortgage loan” has the meaning given the term in .section 5102 of title 12(iv) ServicerThe term “servicer” has the meaning given the term in .section 2605(i) of title 12(B) LimitationIf a person requests a consumer report from a consumer reporting agency in connection with a credit transaction involving a residential mortgage loan, that agency may not, based in whole or in part on that request, furnish a consumer report to another person under this subsection unless—(i) the transaction consists of a firm offer of credit or insurance; and(ii) that other person—(I) has submitted documentation to that agency certifying that such other person has, pursuant to paragraph (1)(A), the authorization of the consumer to whom the consumer report relates; or(II)(aa) has originated a current residential mortgage loan of the consumer to whom the consumer report relates;(bb) is the servicer of a current residential mortgage loan of the consumer to whom the consumer report relates; or(cc)(AA) is an insured depository institution or credit union; and(BB) holds a current account for the consumer to whom the consumer report relates.See 2025 Amendment note below.

A, title II, § 2404(c)110 Stat. 3009–434Sept. 30, 1996“The Federal Trade Commission may issue such guidelines as it deems necessary with respect to the use of consumer reports in connection with insurance transactions that are not initiated by the consumer pursuant to section 604(c) of the Fair Credit Reporting Act [], as added by subsection (a) of this section.”15 U.S.C. 1681b(c)

children.text: In response to the order of a court having jurisdiction to issue such an order, a subpoena issued in connection with proceedings before a Federal grand jury, or a subpoena issued in accordance with or .section 5318 of title 31section 3486 of title 18
In accordance with the written instructions of the consumer to whom it relates.

children.label: A
children.text: intends to use the information in connection with a credit transaction involving the consumer on whom the information is to be furnished and involving the extension of credit to, or review or collection of an account of, the consumer; or

children.label: B
children.text: intends to use the information for employment purposes; or

children.label: C
children.text: intends to use the information in connection with the underwriting of insurance involving the consumer; or

children.label: D
children.text: intends to use the information in connection with a determination of the consumer’s eligibility for a license or other benefit granted by a governmental instrumentality required by law to consider an applicant’s financial responsibility or status; or

children.label: E
children.text: intends to use the information, as a potential investor or servicer, or current insurer, in connection with a valuation of, or an assessment of the credit or prepayment risks associated with, an existing credit obligation; or

children.label: i
children.text: in connection with a business transaction that is initiated by the consumer; or

children.label: ii
children.text: to review an account to determine whether the consumer continues to meet the terms of the account.

children.label: G
children.text: executive departments and agencies in connection with the issuance of government-sponsored individually-billed travel charge cards.

children.label: A
children.text: the consumer report is needed for the purpose of establishing an individual’s capacity to make child support payments, determining the appropriate level of such payments, or enforcing a child support order, award, agreement, or judgment;

children.label: B
children.text: the parentage of the consumer for the child to which the obligation relates has been established or acknowledged by the consumer in accordance with State laws under which the obligation arises (if required by those laws); and

children.label: C
children.text: the consumer report will be kept confidential, will be used solely for a purpose described in subparagraph (A), and will not be used in connection with any other civil, administrative, or criminal proceeding, or for any other purpose.
To an agency administering a State plan under for use to set an initial or modified child support award.section 654 of title 42

To the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation or the National Credit Union Administration as part of its preparation for its appointment or as part of its exercise of powers, as conservator, receiver, or liquidating agent for an insured depository institution or insured credit union under the Federal Deposit Insurance Act [ et seq.] or the Federal Credit Union Act [ et seq.], or other applicable Federal or State law, or in connection with the resolution or liquidation of a failed or failing insured depository institution or insured credit union, as applicable.12 U.S.C. 181112 U.S.C. 1751

children.heading: Conditions for furnishing and using consumer reports for employment purposes
children.label: b Conditions for furnishing and using consumer reports for employment purposes
i
children.text: the person has complied with paragraph (2) with respect to the consumer report, and the person will comply with paragraph (3) with respect to the consumer report if paragraph (3) becomes applicable; and

children.label: ii
children.text: information from the consumer report will not be used in violation of any applicable Federal or State equal employment opportunity law or regulation; and

children.label: B
children.text: the consumer reporting agency provides with the report, or has previously provided, a summary of the consumer’s rights under this subchapter, as prescribed by the Bureau under section 1681g(c)(3) of this title.1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: i
children.text: a clear and conspicuous disclosure has been made in writing to the consumer at any time before the report is procured or caused to be procured, in a document that consists solely of the disclosure, that a consumer report may be obtained for employment purposes; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the consumer has authorized in writing (which authorization may be made on the document referred to in clause (i)) the procurement of the report by that person.

children.heading: Application by mail, telephone, computer, or other similar means
children.label: B Application by mail, telephone, computer, or other similar means
i
children.text: the person who procures the consumer report on the consumer for employment purposes shall provide to the consumer, by oral, written, or electronic means, notice that a consumer report may be obtained for employment purposes, and a summary of the consumer’s rights under section 1681m(a)(3) of this title; and1

children.label: ii
children.text: the consumer shall have consented, orally, in writing, or electronically to the procurement of the report by that person.

children.label: i
children.text: the consumer is applying for a position over which the Secretary of Transportation has the power to establish qualifications and maximum hours of service pursuant to the provisions of , or a position subject to safety regulation by a State transportation agency; andsection 31502 of title 49

children.label: ii
children.text: as of the time at which the person procures the report or causes the report to be procured the only interaction between the consumer and the person in connection with that employment application has been by mail, telephone, computer, or other similar means.

children.heading: Conditions on use for adverse actions
children.label: 3 Conditions on use for adverse actions
i
children.text: a copy of the report; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a description in writing of the rights of the consumer under this subchapter, as prescribed by the Bureau under section 1681g(c)(3) of this title.1

children.heading: Application by mail, telephone, computer, or other similar means
children.label: B Application by mail, telephone, computer, or other similar means
I
children.text: that adverse action has been taken based in whole or in part on a consumer report received from a consumer reporting agency;

children.label: II
children.text: of the name, address and telephone number of the consumer reporting agency that furnished the consumer report (including a toll-free telephone number established by the agency if the agency compiles and maintains files on consumers on a nationwide basis);

children.label: III
children.text: that the consumer reporting agency did not make the decision to take the adverse action and is unable to provide to the consumer the specific reasons why the adverse action was taken; and

children.label: IV
children.text: that the consumer may, upon providing proper identification, request a free copy of a report and may dispute with the consumer reporting agency the accuracy or completeness of any information in a report.

children.label: ii
children.text: If, under clause (B)(i)(IV), the consumer requests a copy of a consumer report from the person who procured the report, then, within 3 business days of receiving the consumer’s request, together with proper identification, the person must send or provide to the consumer a copy of a report and a copy of the consumer’s rights as prescribed by the Bureau under section 1681g(c)(3) of this title.1

children.label: i
children.text: the consumer is applying for a position over which the Secretary of Transportation has the power to establish qualifications and maximum hours of service pursuant to the provisions of , or a position subject to safety regulation by a State transportation agency; andsection 31502 of title 49

children.label: ii
children.text: as of the time at which the person procures the report or causes the report to be procured the only interaction between the consumer and the person in connection with that employment application has been by mail, telephone, computer, or other similar means.

children.label: i
children.text: the consumer report is relevant to a national security investigation of such agency or department;

children.label: ii
children.text: the investigation is within the jurisdiction of such agency or department;

children.label: I
children.text: endanger the life or physical safety of any person;

children.label: II
children.text: result in flight from prosecution;

children.label: III
children.text: result in the destruction of, or tampering with, evidence relevant to the investigation;

children.label: IV
children.text: result in the intimidation of a potential witness relevant to the investigation;

children.label: V
children.text: result in the compromise of classified information; or

children.label: VI
children.text: otherwise seriously jeopardize or unduly delay the investigation or another official proceeding.

children.heading: Notification of consumer upon conclusion of investigation
children.label: B Notification of consumer upon conclusion of investigation
i
children.text: a copy of such consumer report with any classified information redacted as necessary;

children.label: ii
children.text: notice of any adverse action which is based, in part, on the consumer report; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the identification with reasonable specificity of the nature of the investigation for which the consumer report was sought.

children.heading: Delegation by head of agency or department
children.label: C Delegation by head of agency or department
children.text: For purposes of subparagraphs (A) and (B), the head of any agency or department of the United States Government may delegate his or her authorities under this paragraph to an official of such agency or department who has personnel security responsibilities and is a member of the Senior Executive Service or equivalent civilian or military rank.

children.heading: Classified information
children.label: i Classified information
children.text: The term “classified information” means information that is protected from unauthorized disclosure under Executive Order No. 12958 or successor orders.

children.heading: National security investigation
children.label: ii National security investigation
children.text: The term “national security investigation” means any official inquiry by an agency or department of the United States Government to determine the eligibility of a consumer to receive access or continued access to classified information or to determine whether classified information has been lost or compromised.

children.heading: Furnishing reports in connection with credit or insurance transactions that are not initiated by consumer
children.label: c Furnishing reports in connection with credit or insurance transactions that are not initiated by consumer
A
children.text: the consumer authorizes the agency to provide such report to such person; or

children.label: i
children.text: the transaction consists of a firm offer of credit or insurance;

children.label: ii
children.text: the consumer reporting agency has complied with subsection (e);

children.label: iii
children.text: there is not in effect an election by the consumer, made in accordance with subsection (e), to have the consumer’s name and address excluded from lists of names provided by the agency pursuant to this paragraph; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the consumer report does not contain a date of birth that shows that the consumer has not attained the age of 21, or, if the date of birth on the consumer report shows that the consumer has not attained the age of 21, such consumer consents to the consumer reporting agency to such furnishing.

children.heading: Limits on information received under paragraph (1)(B)
children.label: 2 Limits on information received under paragraph (1)(B)
A
children.text: the name and address of a consumer;

children.label: B
children.text: an identifier that is not unique to the consumer and that is used by the person solely for the purpose of verifying the identity of the consumer; and

children.label: C
children.text: other information pertaining to a consumer that does not identify the relationship or experience of the consumer with respect to a particular creditor or other entity.

children.heading: Information regarding inquiries
children.label: 3 Information regarding inquiries
children.text: Except as provided in , a consumer reporting agency shall not furnish to any person a record of inquiries in connection with a credit or insurance transaction that is not initiated by a consumer.section 1681g(a)(5) of this title

children.heading: Election of consumer to be excluded from lists
children.label: e Election of consumer to be excluded from lists

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A consumer may elect to have the consumer’s name and address excluded from any list provided by a consumer reporting agency under subsection (c)(1)(B) in connection with a credit or insurance transaction that is not initiated by the consumer, by notifying the agency in accordance with paragraph (2) that the consumer does not consent to any use of a consumer report relating to the consumer in connection with any credit or insurance transaction that is not initiated by the consumer.

children.label: A
children.text: through the notification system maintained by the agency under paragraph (5); or

children.label: B
children.text: by submitting to the agency a signed notice of election form issued by the agency for purposes of this subparagraph.

children.heading: Response of agency after notification through system
children.label: 3 Response of agency after notification through system
A
children.text: inform the consumer that the election is effective only for the 5-year period following the election if the consumer does not submit to the agency a signed notice of election form issued by the agency for purposes of paragraph (2)(B); and

children.label: B
children.text: provide to the consumer a notice of election form, if requested by the consumer, not later than 5 business days after receipt of the notification of the election through the system established under paragraph (5), in the case of a request made at the time the consumer provides notification through the system.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be effective with respect to a consumer reporting agency beginning 5 business days after the date on which the consumer notifies the agency in accordance with paragraph (2);

children.label: i
children.text: subject to subparagraph (C), during the 5-year period beginning 5 business days after the date on which the consumer notifies the agency of the election, in the case of an election for which a consumer notifies the agency only in accordance with paragraph (2)(A); or

children.label: ii
children.text: until the consumer notifies the agency under subparagraph (C), in the case of an election for which a consumer notifies the agency in accordance with paragraph (2)(B);

children.label: C
children.text: shall not be effective after the date on which the consumer notifies the agency, through the notification system established by the agency under paragraph (5), that the election is no longer effective; and

children.label: D
children.text: shall be effective with respect to each affiliate of the agency.

children.label: i
children.text: establish and maintain a notification system, including a toll-free telephone number, which permits any consumer whose consumer report is maintained by the agency to notify the agency, with appropriate identification, of the consumer’s election to have the consumer’s name and address excluded from any such list of names and addresses provided by the agency for such a transaction; and

children.label: I
children.text: a notification that information in consumer files maintained by the agency may be used in connection with such transactions; and

children.label: II
children.text: the address and toll-free telephone number for consumers to use to notify the agency of the consumer’s election under clause (i).

children.heading: Establishment and maintenance as compliance
children.label: B Establishment and maintenance as compliance
children.text: Establishment and maintenance of a notification system (including a toll-free telephone number) and publication by a consumer reporting agency on the agency’s own behalf and on behalf of any of its affiliates in accordance with this paragraph is deemed to be compliance with this paragraph by each of those affiliates.

children.heading: Notification system by agencies that operate nationwide
children.label: 6 Notification system by agencies that operate nationwide
children.text: Each consumer reporting agency that compiles and maintains files on consumers on a nationwide basis shall establish and maintain a notification system for purposes of paragraph (5) jointly with other such consumer reporting agencies.

children.heading: Certain use or obtaining of information prohibited
children.label: f Certain use or obtaining of information prohibited

children.text: the consumer report is obtained for a purpose for which the consumer report is authorized to be furnished under this section; and
the purpose is certified in accordance with by a prospective user of the report through a general or specific certification.section 1681e of this title

children.label: A
children.text: if furnished in connection with an insurance transaction, the consumer affirmatively consents to the furnishing of the report;

children.label: i
children.text: the information to be furnished is relevant to process or effect the employment or credit transaction; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the consumer provides specific written consent for the furnishing of the report that describes in clear and conspicuous language the use for which the information will be furnished; or

children.label: C
children.text: the information to be furnished pertains solely to transactions, accounts, or balances relating to debts arising from the receipt of medical services, products, or devises, where such information, other than account status or amounts, is restricted or reported using codes that do not identify, or do not provide information sufficient to infer, the specific provider or the nature of such services, products, or devices, as provided in .section 1681c(a)(6) of this title

children.heading: Limitation on creditors
children.label: 2 Limitation on creditors
children.text: Except as permitted pursuant to paragraph (3)(C) or regulations prescribed under paragraph (5)(A), a creditor shall not obtain or use medical information (other than medical information treated in the manner required under ) pertaining to a consumer in connection with any determination of the consumer’s eligibility, or continued eligibility, for credit.section 1681c(a)(6) of this title

children.heading: Actions authorized by Federal law, insurance activities and regulatory determinations
children.label: 3 Actions authorized by Federal law, insurance activities and regulatory determinations
A
children.text: in connection with the business of insurance or annuities, including the activities described in section 18B of the model Privacy of Consumer Financial and Health Information Regulation issued by the National Association of Insurance Commissioners (as in effect on );January 1, 2003

children.label: B
children.text: for any purpose permitted without authorization under the Standards for Individually Identifiable Health Information promulgated by the Department of Health and Human Services pursuant to the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996, or referred to under section 1179 of such Act, or described in ; or1section 6802(e) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: as otherwise determined to be necessary and appropriate, by regulation or order, by the Bureau or the applicable State insurance authority (with respect to any person engaged in providing insurance or annuities).

children.heading: Limitation on redisclosure of medical information
children.label: 4 Limitation on redisclosure of medical information
children.text: Any person that receives medical information pursuant to paragraph (1) or (3) shall not disclose such information to any other person, except as necessary to carry out the purpose for which the information was initially disclosed, or as otherwise permitted by statute, regulation, or order.

children.heading: Regulations and effective date for paragraph (2)
children.label: 5 Regulations and effective date for paragraph (2)

children.heading: Regulations required2So in original. No subpar. (B) has been enacted.2
children.label: A Regulations required2So in original. No subpar. (B) has been enacted.2
children.text: The Bureau may, after notice and opportunity for comment, prescribe regulations that permit transactions under paragraph (2) that are determined to be necessary and appropriate to protect legitimate operational, transactional, risk, consumer, and other needs (and which shall include permitting actions necessary for administrative verification purposes), consistent with the intent of paragraph (2) to restrict the use of medical information for inappropriate purposes.

children.heading: Coordination with other laws
children.label: 6 Coordination with other laws
children.text: No provision of this subsection shall be construed as altering, affecting, or superseding the applicability of any other provision of Federal law relating to medical confidentiality.
